changes.description: Persistent config system (config.json) for secondary dongle assignment — meteor detector, ADS-B, or WEFAX can be assigned to dongle 2 via UI dropdown or /api/config/secondary endpoint
Secondary dongle config UI in header — dropdown selector with status indicator, persists across restarts
Signal classifier and SEI panels wired into main UI (index.html) — always-visible when receiving audio, hidden on WEFAX/Science/ADS-B-only tabs
Version badge displayed in UI header from backend VERSION constant
WEFAX receiver: added 2s startup health check for rtl_fm process, robust finally-block cleanup to prevent orphaned processes holding USB device
WEFAX receiver: build_rtl_fm_cmd now instance method using self.device_index for multi-dongle support

WEFAX scheduler: expanded priority chart types to include 48hr, 96hr, 144hr forecasts and wave charts; widened trigger window from ±10s to ±60s to prevent missed broadcasts; added dedup set to prevent double-triggers
InputSource enter_apt_mode/enter_wefax_mode: always stop source to release USB device, even if not marked as running
App startup: meteor detector and ADS-B stopped before WEFAX recording in single-dongle mode to prevent device contention
Audio-related UI sections (signal, stats, classifier, SEI, controls) hidden on non-audio tabs (WEFAX, Science, ADS-B only, AIS only)

Replaced rtl_fm subprocess with pyrtlsdr direct IQ capture as main audio pipeline. Raw IQ samples now flow to signal classifier, IQ segmenter, SEI emitter fingerprinter, and live spectrogram waterfall in real time. Demodulated PCM audio still flows to Whisper transcriber and browser audio stream unchanged.
Dual-path Tuner: auto-detects pyrtlsdr at startup, delegates all methods to IQCapture when available, falls back to rtl_fm subprocess unchanged if pyrtlsdr not installed

Python DSP pipeline in iq_capture.py: FM demod (conjugate product discriminator), AM demod (envelope detection), WFM demod, squelch gate (RMS thresholding), de-emphasis filter (75μs single-pole IIR), frequency string parser
Live IQ pipeline wiring in app.py: IQSegmenter detects transmission boundaries every chunk, signal classifier runs every 500ms, spectrogram_row Socket.IO event emitted every 300ms for waterfall UI
Spectrogram waterfall now renders live data — classifier.js binds spectrogram_row event to existing _renderWaterfall() method
InputSource.set_iq_callback() passthrough for wiring raw IQ from Tuner to classifier pipeline
Environment variables CLASSIFIER_HEF_PATH, CLASSIFIER_CLASSES_PATH, SEI_HEF_PATH for NPU model deployment without code changes

NPU model training guide (operations/npu-model-training.md): Mac ARM64 training workflow, 7-step classifier pipeline, 6-step SEI pipeline, HEF compilation via Docker/DFC, deployment instructions, troubleshooting
IQ capture sample rate reduced from 2.4 MHz to 240 kHz (matches rtl_fm 200k bandwidth, reduces CPU load on Pi 5)
setup.sh: added pip install pyrtlsdr, signal classifier and SEI data directories
Unit tests: 34 new tests for DSP functions (freq parsing, FM/AM/WFM demod, squelch, de-emphasis, PCM conversion, IQCapture interface)
Meteor detector, APT decoder, WEFAX receiver unchanged — keep independent rtl_fm instances (different sample rates, pipe to sox/fldigi)

Phase 16 — Signal Classification via Spectrogram CNN: real-time modulation identification (AM, FM, WFM, SSB, P25, DMR, ADS-B, NOAA APT, WEFAX, CW) from IQ spectrograms using MobileNetV2 on Hailo-8L NPU with CPU heuristic fallback

signal_classifier.py — IQ-to-spectrogram conversion (256-point FFT, Hann window, 50% overlap), spectrogram normalization to 224x224 uint8, Hailo inference wrapper, confidence thresholding (>0.7), uncertainty detection, self-supervised sample collection
iq_capture.py — direct IQ capture via pyrtlsdr bypassing rtl_fm, FM/AM demodulation from raw IQ, device mutex to prevent concurrent access with rtl_fm

ML training pipeline (code/ml/signal_classifier/) — RadioML 2018.01A dataset loader, MobileNetV2 fine-tuning with differential learning rates, ONNX export, Hailo DFC compilation for HAILO8L, model evaluation with confusion matrix

Signal classification panel UI (classifier.js + classifier.css) — always-visible panel with current modulation type, confidence bar, spectrogram waterfall canvas, scrolling classification history, accuracy tracker vs preset ground truth
expected_modulation field added to all 19 frequency presets for ground truth comparison and self-supervised labeling
GET /api/classifier/status endpoint and signal_classified Socket.IO event

Phase 17 — Specific Emitter Identification (SEI): passive RF fingerprinting via 1D CNN + attention on raw IQ, 128-dimensional embedding vectors, cosine similarity matching against local emitter database, automatic enrollment of new emitters

sei_model.py — Hailo-8L inference for 128-dim embeddings, cosine similarity matcher (threshold 0.85), emitter database with atomic JSON persistence, exponential moving average centroid updates, sequential ID assignment (EMITTER-001, etc)
iq_segmenter.py — transmission boundary detection from continuous IQ stream using power thresholding with hysteresis, ring buffer, SNR estimation, min/max duration filtering (50ms–30s)
SEI ML training pipeline (code/ml/sei/) — ADS-B-labeled IQ collection script, 1D CNN + SE attention triplet loss training, ONNX export, Hailo DFC compilation, rank-1/rank-5 accuracy evaluation

Emitter tracking panel UI (sei.js + sei.css) — always-visible panel with known emitters table (sortable, inline label editing), re-identification feed with ID/NEW badges, new emitter alerts, database statistics
GET /api/emitters (paginated), GET /api/emitters/<id>, POST /api/emitters/<id>/label, GET /api/sei/status endpoints and emitter_identified, new_emitter Socket.IO events
Classifier-to-SEI pipeline wiring: classify_segment() method, _forward_to_sei() with confidence/SNR/duration gating
Unit tests: 21 signal classifier tests + 10 IQ segmenter tests + 22 SEI model tests. Integration tests: 7 classifier pipeline + 7 SEI pipeline (67 new tests total)
Phase 14 — WEFAX Weather Fax: HF direct sampling receiver (-D 2 Q-branch), fldigi WEFAX decode via Xvfb, NMC/NOJ broadcast scheduler with time-of-day frequency selection, chart history and filter UI
wefax_scheduler.py — hardcoded NMC Point Reyes and NOJ Kodiak schedules, 5-min advance Socket.IO notification, priority tagging for surface analysis and 24hr forecast charts
wefax_receiver.py — rtl_fm USB demod with -1.9 kHz offset, sox WAV pipeline, fldigi headless decode, structured PNG filename output, image history API
WEFAX panel UI (wefax.js + wefax.css) — broadcast schedule with station/frequency badges, countdown, active reception indicator, decoded chart display, chart type filter buttons, thumbnail history
InputSource WEFAX mode — enter/exit with preset restore, APT satellite pass priority enforcement
GET /api/wefax/latest, /api/wefax/schedule, /api/wefax/history endpoints and wefax_broadcast_upcoming, wefax_image_ready Socket.IO events
Phase 15 — Passive Meteor Scatter Detection: forward scatter carrier power monitor, threshold-based burst detection, underdense/overdense trail classification, shower calendar correlation
meteor_detector.py — IQ power monitoring via rtl_fm, rolling 30s baseline, configurable dB threshold, duration filtering (50ms–30s), trail type classification, JSON event log
meteor_analyzer.py — 7-shower annual calendar (Quadrantids through Geminids), active shower tagging, hourly/daily rate stats, session statistics with peak rate and U/O ratio
Meteor panel UI (meteor.js + meteor.css) — real-time event feed with trail badges, 24hr rate bar chart on canvas, shower context, stats grid, target frequency display
Dual dongle meteor mode (METEOR_DUAL_DONGLE=true) — device 1 runs 24/7 independent of main pipeline. Single dongle mode at background priority
GET /api/meteor/events (paginated, filterable), /api/meteor/stats, /api/meteor/showers endpoints and meteor_detection, meteor_stats_update Socket.IO events
setup.sh — added fldigi and Xvfb install steps for WEFAX headless decode, WEFAX directory creation, HF antenna guidance
Unit tests: 49 WEFAX tests (scheduler frequency selection, schedule parsing, receiver commands) + 47 meteor tests (threshold, classification, shower calendar, rate stats)
RTL-SDR Blog V4 driver — stock Debian librtlsdr causes PLL not locked on R828D tuner. Setup now builds patched driver from rtlsdrblog/rtl-sdr-blog, reinstalls after dump1090

Whisper decode prefix — Hailo NPU decoder seeded with full prefix (<|startoftranscript|> <|en|> <|transcribe|> <|notimestamps|>) instead of just start token. Decoder now produces real transcriptions (28/32 tokens)
Signal meter flickering — heartbeat loop no longer emits rms:0 every 500ms, only resets on stop transition
ADS-B scan scheduler — uses eventlet.spawn instead of threading.Thread to avoid greenlet cross-thread errors
ADS-B scans only activate on Aviation presets, no longer interrupt Weather/Broadcast/Marine
NOAA Weather presets changed from wbfm to fm (narrowband FM)
Antenna guide (operations/antenna-guide.md) — element lengths, orientation diagrams, positioning, PLL troubleshooting
System diagram (operations/system-diagram.md) — physical setup, software architecture, data flow, hardware stack
Squelch & Gain tooltip info icons, satellite panel Weather-only visibility, audio auto-stop on source stop

Phase 12 complete — NOAA weather radio integration: continuous 162.550 MHz monitoring, NWS broadcast transcript parser (temperature, wind, visibility, alerts, marine forecasts), weather conditions panel UI with priority alert banners, 30s squelch-zero segmenter, weather preset
noaa_parser.py — structured NWS broadcast parser: regex extraction for temperature, wind, humidity, visibility, marine forecasts, and priority alerts (warnings/watches)
weather.js — WeatherPanel class with live conditions display, priority alert banners (pulsing red), marine forecast section, auto-refresh on weather_update Socket.IO events
Continuous capture mode — 30s audio segmenter for squelch-zero NOAA broadcasts, weather callback pipeline from transcriber to parser to UI

Phase 13 complete — NOAA APT satellite imaging: auto-scheduled pass recording for NOAA-15/19 at 137 MHz, orbital prediction with ephem + Celestrak TLEs, rtl_fm wideband FM capture at 60k sample rate, noaa-apt CLI decoding to PNG
apt_scheduler.py — TLE-based pass prediction using PyEphem, 24h lookahead, 20° minimum elevation filter, Celestrak TLE refresh (24h cache), background scheduling loop with 10-min advance notification
apt_decoder.py — APT pass recorder using rtl_fm + sox pipeline, 11025 Hz sample rate, noaa-apt CLI decode with auto-rotate, decoded PNG storage, image history tracking

satellite.js + satellite.css — SatellitePanel UI: upcoming pass list with elevation indicators, countdown timer, recording progress bar, decoded image display with click-to-expand, image history thumbnails
InputSource APT mode — enter_apt_mode/exit_apt_mode methods for exclusive SDR access during satellite passes, automatic preset restore after recording
GET /api/satellite/passes and /api/satellite/latest-image endpoints for satellite pass schedule and decoded image retrieval
Unit tests for NOAA parser (24 tests), APT scheduler (TLE parsing, pass prediction, elevation filtering), APT decoder (command construction, state management), integration test for APT pipeline
setup.sh — added ephem pip install, noaa-apt CLI install (apt or Rust build), APT directory creation (/tmp/ravensdr/apt/, static/images/apt/)
requirements.txt — added ephem>=4.1.0 for orbital mechanics
Project scaffolding — directory structure, state files, architecture docs, implementation plans
Input source abstraction — Tuner (rtl_fm) and StreamSource (ffmpeg) with auto-detection
Transcriber with faster-whisper CPU fallback (whisper-tiny, int8)
Audio router — HTTP chunked WAV streaming to browser
Flask backend — REST API routes + Socket.IO events for transcripts and signal levels
Console UI — single-page web frontend with preset selector, signal meter, audio player, transcript feed
Frequency presets for Seattle/Redmond area (weather, aviation, marine, public safety, broadcast)
Setup script (setup.sh) — system deps, venv, RTL-SDR, Hailo SDK detection
Hailo-8L NPU inference — mel.py spectrogram, encoder/decoder HEF pipeline via VDevice/InferModel API, 10s chunks, iterative decoder with repetition penalty, tokenizer decode
download_models.sh — fetches encoder/decoder HEF + token embedding npy from Hailo S3
setup.sh — hailo_platform symlink into venv, auto-download model files when Hailo NPU detected
requirements.txt — added torch, transformers dependencies for Hailo inference
Transcriber chunk size from 3s (48k samples) to 10s (160k samples) to match Hailo encoder input
AGPL-3.0 license — copyleft requiring source sharing for modifications and network use
Attributions dashboard page — full dependency table with GitHub repos, licenses, and copyright notices for all third-party projects
README updated with AGPL-3.0 license section and attributions table listing all 13+ dependencies
.nojekyll file to fix GitHub Pages serving of .state/ JSON files
Dashboard navigation — added Attributions link to all pages
Phase 10 implementation plan — ADS-B flight tracking with dump1090, callsign-to-flight correlation from Whisper transcripts, Leaflet.js map panel, single/dual dongle modes
Voice-activity audio segmentation plan — silence-boundary chunking replaces fixed 10s splits, prevents cutting words mid-utterance
Phase 11 implementation plan — inference stats dashboard with encoder/decoder timing, RTF, tokens/s, silence skip rate, frontend stats panel
Phase 8 complete — SDR disconnect detection with auto-recovery on replug, process crash auto-restart (3 retries), /api/retry endpoint, error banner with retry button in UI
Phase 11 complete — inference stats dashboard with encoder/decoder timing, RTF, tokens/s, decoder steps, chunks processed, silence skip rate in UI. GET /api/stats endpoint, periodic 5s broadcast.

Phase 12 implementation plan — NOAA weather radio integration: continuous 162.550 MHz monitoring, NWS broadcast transcript parser (temperature, wind, visibility, alerts, marine forecasts), weather panel UI with priority alert banners, squelch-zero continuous capture mode

Phase 13 implementation plan — NOAA APT satellite imaging: auto-scheduled pass recording for NOAA-15/19 at 137 MHz using ephem + Celestrak TLEs, rtl_fm capture, noaa-apt CLI decoding, satellite panel UI with pass countdown, decoded image display, and image history
